advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase before cross-department learning begins and next phase promotion is granted.
key_learnings: Dietary patterns outperform single nutrients for cardiovascular protection — the Mediterranean and DASH diets reduce CVD risk by 25–30%. Replacing saturated fat with unsaturated fats (especially polyunsaturated) reduces LDL cholesterol and cardiovascular events. Dietary sodium restriction (below 2.3 g/day) is effective for blood pressure management, particularly in salt-sensitive individuals.
research_findings: Every 1% of energy from saturated fat replaced by polyunsaturated fat reduces coronary heart disease risk by approximately 2–3%. Plant sterols at 2g/day reduce LDL cholesterol by 5–10% without medications. The PREDIMED study showed an olive oil-enriched Mediterranean diet reduced major cardiovascular events by 30% compared to the control group.
current_focus_areas: Developing lipid-lowering dietary protocols beyond statin therapy; building sodium tracking tools for hypertension management; researching the role of polyphenols, flavonoids, and nitrate-rich vegetables in vascular health.

learning_log.entry: Assigned to the Performance & Metabolic Nutrition department under Department Leader Bot 9 (Sports Nutrition). Received learning_start_directive: begin independent learning immediately and reach 100% before cross-department study begins. Reporting daily to Copilot as part of the Diet Specialist Team. Progress: 53%.
